perturbation.original_text: Confidential information that is disclosed orally will be confirmed in writing by the disclosing party within thirty (30) days after such disclosure. The parties agree that such written confirmation when mailed by the disclosing party, will form a part of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary information that is disclosed orally will be confirmed in writing by the disclosing party within thirty (30) days after such disclosure. The parties agree that such written confirmation when mailed by the disclosing party, will form a part of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential information" to "Proprietary information" creates uncertainty. The agreement defines 'Confidential information' but not 'Proprietary information'. This leads to a contradiction and confusion because the agreement uses the term 'Confidential Information' frequently but the text specifies that 'Proprietary information' needs to be confirmed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Confidential information" is any information disclosed in any form whatsoever, tangible or intangible including, but not limited to, a device, sample, material, product, graphic, printed, written, drawing, chart, diagram, sketch, notes, figure, machine-recognizable form including data stored in electronic storage devices of all types, or other tangible form, audio disks, tapes and cassettes, video disks, tapes and cassettes, electronic transmission of all types, including radio, television, satellite, cable, telephone, or information disclosed orally or visually, or information disclosed in other forms, or information disclosed in other forms developed in the future to the receiving party (either COMPANY or FieldWorks) areas and that is marked, designated, labeled or identified at the time of disclosure as being confidential or its equivalent. Proprietary information that is disclosed orally will be confirmed in writing by the disclosing party within thirty (30) days after such disclosure. The parties agree that such written confirmation when mailed by the disclosing party, will form a part of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential information' to 'Proprietary information' creates a contradiction because the contract defines 'Confidential information' but not 'Proprietary information'. The contradicted text also uses the term 'Confidential Information' but the changed text specifies that 'Proprietary information' needs to be confirmed in writing, thus creating a contradiction. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise expressly authorized by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees to retain the confidential information in confidence for the "Confidential Period" defined in paragraph number 3 below, during which period the receiving party shall not disclose the confidential information to any third party, and shall not use the confidential information for any purpose other than the aforesaid purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise expressly authorized by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees to retain the protected data in confidence for the "Confidential Period" defined in paragraph number 3 below, during which period the receiving party shall not disclose the confidential information to any third party, and shall not use the confidential information for any purpose other than the aforesaid purposes.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "confidential information" to "protected data" here creates an issue, because the term `protected data` is nowhere defined, thus raising questions about whether all confidential information is regarded as `protected data`, which, due to the lack of definition, creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise expressly authorized by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees to retain the protected data in confidence for the "Confidential Period" defined in paragraph number 3 below, during which period the receiving party shall not disclose the confidential information to any third party, and shall not use the confidential information for any purpose other than the aforesaid purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'confidential information,' which is assumed to be a defined term. The changed text replaces 'confidential information' with 'protected data' in one instance, while leaving 'confidential information' in another. The Perturbation Explanation states that `protected data` is nowhere defined. The Contradicted Text contains both `protected data` and `confidential information`, mirroring the Changed Text. This creates a contradiction because the contract uses the terms 'protected data' and 'confidential information' seemingly interchangeably without defining what `protected data` means. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition because the multiple terms are used interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations or ambiguity about which information is subject to which obligation.

perturbation.original_text: The "Confidential Period" shall mean two (2) years from the date of receipt of the confidential information or until such time as the information no longer qualifies as confidential information pursuant to paragraph number 5 below.
perturbation.changed_text: The "Confidential Period" shall mean one (1) year from the date of receipt of the proprietary data or until such time as the information no longer qualifies as confidential information pursuant to paragraph number 5 below.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "two (2) years" to "one (1) year" and "confidential information" to "proprietary data" here creates an internal contradiction. As defined, the time period refers to the `Confidential Period`. Yet in practice, it now seems to be linked to `proprietary data`, which is not defined anywhere.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The "Confidential Period" shall mean one (1) year from the date of receipt of the proprietary data or until such time as the information no longer qualifies as confidential information pursuant to paragraph number 5 below.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Confidential Period' definition by reducing the duration from two years to one year and replacing 'confidential information' with 'proprietary data'. The contradicted text also defines 'Confidential Period' using 'proprietary data' and a one-year timeframe. While both texts are the same, the contradiction exists because the changed text uses 'proprietary data', which is not explicitly defined in the contract (as per the Perturbation Explanation), while the original text uses 'confidential information', which aligns with paragraph 5. This misalignment of terminology, specifically the use of the undefined 'proprietary data', creates an in-text contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall limit dissemination of the confidential information to such of its employees or agents who have a need to know for the aforesaid purposes. 4.1. Further, the receiving party agrees to instruct all such employees and agents not to disclose such confidential information to third parties. Each such employee and agent shall be individually bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall limit dissemination of the company information to such of its employees or agents who have a need to know for the aforesaid purposes. 4.1. Further, the receiving party agrees to instruct all such employees and agents not to disclose such confidential information to third parties. Each such employee and agent shall be individually bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "confidential information" to "company information" introduces a contradiction because 'company information' is not defined within the agreement. It is therefore uncertain whether this term encompasses all that is defined under `confidential information`.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party shall limit dissemination of the company information to such of its employees or agents who have a need to know for the aforesaid purposes. 4.1. Further, the receiving party agrees to instruct all such employees and agents not to disclose such confidential information to third parties. Each such employee and agent shall be individually bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'confidential information,' which is presumably defined elsewhere in the agreement. The changed text replaces this with 'company information' in one instance while retaining 'confidential information' in another. This creates a contradiction, because 'company information' is not defined, thus making it unclear whether 'company information' includes the same scope of information that 'confidential information' covers, especially since the same sentence references them both.

perturbation.original_text: All confidential information shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Further, the receiving party agrees to return to the disclosing party, upon request, any information disclosed in any tangible form, all copies thereof, containing any of the confidential information referred to in paragraph number 1 above.
perturbation.changed_text: All sensitive data shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Further, the receiving party agrees to return to the disclosing party, upon request, any information disclosed in any tangible form, all copies thereof, containing any of the confidential information referred to in paragraph number 1 above.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "confidential information" to "sensitive data" creates a contradiction. The term 'sensitive data' is not defined in the contract, leading to ambiguity as to what constitutes 'sensitive data' and whether it aligns with the definition of 'confidential information'. This may cause confusion as to what must be returned to the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All sensitive data shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Further, the receiving party agrees to return to the disclosing party, upon request, any information disclosed in any tangible form, all copies thereof, containing any of the confidential information referred to in paragraph number 1 above.
perturbation.justification: The change from "confidential information" to "sensitive data" introduces a contradiction because 'sensitive data' is undefined, while 'confidential information' is defined in paragraph 1. The contradicted text refers to 'confidential information', but the changed text only mentions 'sensitive data'. Since the modified text uses a different term that is not defined and is meant to mean the same as 'confidential information', it creates ambiguity and potential conflict regarding what information the receiving party is obligated to return, leading to an in-text contradiction as per the definition of Misaligned Terminology.
